scope: custom: combat -- R10 duel-vs-battlefield resolution context (armature reset 2026-05-29)
note: Resolves the weapon-convergence thread per Jordan's battlefield insight. Strikes-to-fell analysis proved the war hammer's duel dominance is DAMAGE-driven (fells in ~2-3 strikes vs blades' 4-6, a 2x armour-defeating advantage sigma-tempo cannot offset) -- so it cannot be made duel-weak by any tempo channel, AND SHOULD NOT BE: it is a BATTLEFIELD weapon (poleaxe/war-hammer/maul), used against an armoured foe ALREADY ENGAGED with a third party (tied up, cannot defend/reposition). This module models the two CONTEXTS rather than balancing one weapon across both: DUEL (R8/R9, full defence -- the 7 dueling weapons cluster ~29pp spread; war hammer excluded from the duel-balance target) vs BATTLEFIELD (defensive sigma-leverage SUPPRESSED -- engaged foe cannot defend; raw armour-defeating damage governs; war hammer dominates -- fells engaged-armoured foe in 2.3 strikes vs longsword 4.3, dagger 6.3, advantage GROWS with armour, the inverse of its duel weakness). Grounded bottom-up (canonical damage + weapon-vs-armour, combat_v30 §5; the duel channels this suppresses) + top-down (Jordan's battlefield insight + historical poleaxe role). Gap-completion: gives heavy weapons the context canon intends, NOT a nerf to canonical damage. Reuses R1/R2/M3. Class-C = battlefield defence-suppression factor. PROPOSAL -- Jordan-vetoable; no canon file edited.

entries.quoted_text: Fraction of defensive sigma-leverage an ENGAGED foe retains; 0 = fully occupied by a third party (the battlefield press). The duel's defensive channels (reaction, facing, bind contest, declining the exchange) are canonical; suppressing them for an engaged foe is the battlefield-context model.
A strike on an undefended/occupied foe resolves at an easier Ob (2, Routine-Difficult) than a contested duel sub-action (3). Ob scale canonical.
Opportunistic strikes a battlefield attacker lands on an engaged foe before the tableau shifts; harness parameter for the battlefield context, not a canonical constant.
